Package org.eventb.core

Interface IPRProof

All Superinterfaces:
org.eclipse.core.runtime.IAdaptable, IElementManipulation, IInternalElement, IParent, IPRProofInfoElement, IRodinElement, ISnapshotable
All Known Implementing Classes:
PRProof
public interface IPRProof extends IInternalElement, IPRProofInfoElement
Common protocol for Event-B Proof elements stored in the RODIN Database.

This is intended to be the top-most interface for serializing and deserializing proof data structures. This means that it is not intended that database elements beyond this point be seen or manipulated. Note also that this interface provides a more abstract view of the database by hiding the internal database representation.

This interface is meant to be used as follows. An instance of IProofTree may be serialized into the database using the setProofTree(IProofTree, IProgressMonitor) method. This serializes the formula factory, the proof skeleton (IProofSkeleton) and proof dependencies IProofDependencies of this proof tree which may later be deserialized using the getFormulaFactory(IProgressMonitor), getSkeleton(FormulaFactory, IProgressMonitor) and getProofDependencies(FormulaFactory, IProgressMonitor) methods.

The proof dependencies are used to check if the proof is applicable to any proof obligation. The proof skeleton can be used to rebuild the proof tree.

All predicates and expressions used in a proof are gathered in this element. Hence this element contains also the following children:

  • Identifiers occurring free in a predicate or expression in the proof.
  • Predicates used in the proof.
  • Expressions used in the proof.
Moreover, the proof also carries an attribute called org.eventb.core.prSets which contains a list of all carrier sets used in the proof (set names separated by commas).

Clients should use the Proof Manager API rather than direct access to this Rodin database API.

Since:
1.0
See Also:
  • IProofTree
  • IProofDependencies
  • IProofSkeleton
  • IProofManager

  • Field Details

    • ELEMENT_TYPE

      static final IInternalElementType<IPRProof> ELEMENT_TYPE

  • Method Details

    • getConfidence

      int getConfidence() throws RodinDBException
      Returns the confidence of proof tree stored in this proof element.

      Note that this method returns IConfidence.UNATTEMPTED if no proof is currently stored in this proof element.

      Specified by:
      getConfidence in interface IPRProofInfoElement
      Returns:
      the confidence of this proof tree
      Throws:
      RodinDBException

    • getHasManualProof

      boolean getHasManualProof() throws RodinDBException
      Returns whether this proof obligation has been discharged manually. A proof obligation is considered as manually discharged if the end user entered manually its associated proof (even partially).

      The returned value is true iff the corresponding attribute contains true. Hence, if the attribute is absent, false is returned.

      Specified by:
      getHasManualProof in interface IPRProofInfoElement
      Returns:
      true if the user contributed to the proof of this proof obligation
      Throws:
      RodinDBException
      See Also:
      • setHasManualProof(boolean, IProgressMonitor)

    • setHasManualProof

      void setHasManualProof(boolean value, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Sets whether this proof obligation has been discharged manually.
      Specified by:
      setHasManualProof in interface IPRProofInfoElement
      Parameters:
      value - The value to set to
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException
      See Also:
      • getHasManualProof()

    • setProofTree

      void setProofTree(IProofTree proofTree, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Sets the proof tree of this proof element by serializing the given proof tree into the database. The proof tree can be modified by pruning some nodes in case of serialization failure.

      This method also increments the PR stamp for this element, as well as the file that contains it.

      Parameters:
      proofTree - The proof tree to set
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException

    • getProofTree

      IProofTree getProofTree(org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Returns a proof tree for this proof. The proof tree is built only with information local to this proof. The root sequent of the proof tree is made of the needed hypotheses and goal (if any) of this proof. Then, all rules contained in this proof are applied successively to reconstruct the proof tree saved in this proof.

      No attempt is made to access the corresponding proof obligation. No reasoner is run.

      Parameters:
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Returns:
      the proof tree, or null if the proof tree could not be rebuilt.
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if there was a problem accessing the Rodin database, or while computing the proof tree for the current proof

    • getFormulaFactory

      FormulaFactory getFormulaFactory(org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Returns the formula factory of this proof. The returned factory can be used subsequently to parse the proof dependencies and proof skeleton of this proof.
      Parameters:
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Returns:
      the formula factory to use for parsing this proof
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if a problem occurs while deserializing
      Since:
      3.0

    • getProofDependencies

      IProofDependencies getProofDependencies(FormulaFactory factory, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Returns the proof dependencies for proof tree stored in this proof element. Normally, the given factory should be the result of getFormulaFactory(IProgressMonitor). Optionally, another factory can be used but it is up to the caller to ensure that it will be able to parse the stored proof.

      In case no proof tree is stored in this proof element, this method returns the broadest result (i.e. without any dependency)

      Parameters:
      factory - the formula factory to use
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Returns:
      The proof dependencies for proof tree stored in this proof element
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if there was a problem accessing the database, or computing + the proof dependencies

    • getSkeleton

      IProofSkeleton getSkeleton(FormulaFactory factory, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Returns the proof skeleton of the proof tree stored in this proof element. Normally, the given factory should be the result of getFormulaFactory(IProgressMonitor). Optionally, another factory can be used but it is up to the caller to ensure that it will be able to parse the stored proof.
      Parameters:
      factory - the formula factory to use
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Returns:
      the stored proof skeleton
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if a problem occurred when accessing the database, or if the proof skeleton is invalid

    • getIdentifier

      IPRIdentifier getIdentifier(String name)
      Returns a handle to the identifier child with the given name. That child element describes an identifier that occurs free in the proof.

      This is a handle-only method. The identifier element may or may not be present.

      Parameters:
      name - name of the child
      Returns:
      a handle to the child identifier with the given name
      See Also:
      • getIdentifiers()

    • getIdentifiers

      IPRIdentifier[] getIdentifiers() throws RodinDBException
      Returns all children identifier elements. Those child elements describe all identifiers that occur free in the proof (and are not introduced as fresh identifiers by the proof itself).
      Returns:
      an array of all chidren element of type identifier
      Throws:
      RodinDBException
      See Also:
      • getIdentifier(String)

    • getExpression

      IPRStoredExpr getExpression(String name)
      Returns a handle to the expression child with the given name.

      This is a handle-only method. The expression element may or may not be present.

      Parameters:
      name - element name of the child
      Returns:
      a handle to the child expression with the given element name
      See Also:
      • getExpressions()

    • getExpressions

      IPRStoredExpr[] getExpressions() throws RodinDBException
      Returns all children expression elements.
      Returns:
      an array of all chidren element of type expression
      Throws:
      RodinDBException
      See Also:
      • getExpression(String)

    • getPredicate

      IPRStoredPred getPredicate(String name)
      Returns a handle to the predicate child with the given name.

      This is a handle-only method. The predicate element may or may not be present.

      Parameters:
      name - element name of the child
      Returns:
      a handle to the child predicate with the given element name
      See Also:
      • getPredicates()

      IPRStoredPred[] getPredicates() throws RodinDBException
      Returns all children predicate elements.
      Returns:
      an array of all chidren element of type predicate
      Throws:
      RodinDBException
      See Also:
      • getPredicate(String)

    • getSets

      String[] getSets() throws RodinDBException
      Returns the carrier sets that are used in this proof.
      Returns:
      an array of the carrier set names of this proof
      Throws:
      RodinDBException
      See Also:

    • setSets

      void setSets(String[] sets, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Sets the carrier sets that are used in this proof.
      Parameters:
      sets - the carrier set names to set
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException

    • getProofRule

      IPRProofRule getProofRule(String name)
      Returns a handle to the proof rule child with the given name.

      This is a handle-only method. The proof rule element may or may not be present.

      Parameters:
      name - element name of the child
      Returns:
      a handle to the child proof rule with the given element name
      See Also:
      • getProofRules()

    • getProofRules

      IPRProofRule[] getProofRules() throws RodinDBException
      Returns all children proof rule elements. In a well-formed file, this array should contain only one element.
      Returns:
      an array of all chidren element of type proof rule
      Throws:
      RodinDBException
      See Also:
      • getProofRule(String)

    • getReasoner

      IPRReasoner getReasoner(String name)
      Returns a handle to the reasoner child with the given name.

      This is a handle-only method. The reasoner element may or may not be present.

      Parameters:
      name - element name of the child
      Returns:
      a handle to the child reasoner with the given element name
      Since:
      2.2

    • getReasoners

      IPRReasoner[] getReasoners() throws RodinDBException
      Returns all reasoner child elements.
      Returns:
      an array of all used reasoners
      Throws:
      RodinDBException
      Since:
      2.2
      See Also:
      • getPredicate(String)
